stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The content relates directly to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates relevance to the leading edge of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics.
Addresses the critical gap of manual, expertise-intensive PCB design by enabling generation from natural-language requests.
Overcomes key challenges related to the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The corpus is designed for visual generative modeling and features 100M training examples captioned by a state-of-the-art vision-language model.
Crucially, all images in GPIC are permissively licensed for both research and commercial use, addressing data accessibility concerns.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformer architectures and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads for the platform.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
It introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This work is crucial for post-hoc auditing, helping researchers understand and diagnose the behavioral characteristics and failure modes of LLMs.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable method to measure the deviation from a theoretically coherent state based on system output and coupling constraints.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses the limitation of current pipelines that rely on static visual encoders, pushing motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.
The framework utilizes heterogeneous data (image-language-3D flow triplets) to better capture action-relevant scene aspects for robotics.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.
This work provides a novel method for post-hoc auditing of LLM behavior by inferring its 'digital DNA' (training data composition).

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes challenges related to the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test if LLMs can assess the methodological quality of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research: judging the viability of a hypothesis before resource-intensive experimentation.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 ML research proposals sourced from ICLR submissions.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only generated text.
This research offers a novel approach for post-hoc auditing and understanding the behavioral characteristics and failure modes of proprietary LLMs.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes challenges related to the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global logical consistency.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and predefined cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for clinical reasoning by moving beyond static datasets.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a new dataset pipeline that generates clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Enables controllable benchmarking of LLMs in settings that mimic real-world Electronic Health Record (EHR) interoperability.

stories.title: Building AshaPulse — An AI-Powered Health Assistant for India's Frontline Warriors
stories.author: priyanshu79
bullet_points: Development of AshaPulse, an AI-powered health assistant specifically designed for frontline workers in India.
The system emphasizes offline diagnostic capabilities, crucial for rural and underserved healthcare settings.
The project addresses a significant real-world problem by leveraging AI for accessible healthcare delivery in India.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates native support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tooling and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is under scrutiny regarding its management of zero-day exploit disclosures.
A figure named 'Nightmare Eclipse' is publicly demonstrating exploit code, potentially linked to a former employee.
The focus of the story is on Microsoft's response to these public exploit disclosures, as noted by cybersecurity researcher Kevin Beaumont.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story falls into the category of cybersecurity threat mitigation.

stories.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A startup is offering free home cleaning services in exchange for data collection.
The core requirement is that workers must wear head cameras to record activities for robot training.
This represents a new iteration of using human labor to generate data for AI/robotics development.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) in scientific software development, specifically in physics.
A physicist supervised the AI over 12 days to build a differentiable one-loop perturbation theory module in JAX.
The research quantifies supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved many tasks, supplemented by the physicist's domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the memory and latency associated with the KV cache in video generation.
Replaces traditional per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention mechanism (MLA).

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in applying large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to time-series anomaly detection.
Highlights the limitation of current benchmarks, which lack natural-language rationales for anomalies.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to enable grounded and interpretable decisions from VLMs.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLM reasoning where intermediate steps are tied to autoregressive token generation.
The core idea is to mimic human cognition by using internal 'working memory' instead of externalizing thoughts.
This aims to decouple internal computation from external communication to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base model, can elicit strong reasoning capabilities.
This method potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training steps like Reinforcement Learning (RL) or curated datasets.
The primary challenge for practical application is developing an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this power distribution.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) for Large Language Models (LLMs), which currently requires slow, per-query adaptation.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a new method designed to make TTFT practical and fast by optimizing the selection and finetuning processes.
The research focuses on balancing the trade-off between the speed of retrieval/selection and the quality/diversity of the model adaptation.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding biased and unstable model training.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to better account for unequal and time-varying client contributions.
Focuses on improving fairness and stability within the privacy-preserving FL paradigm.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for advanced reasoning models.
It suggests that sampling from a 'power distribution' of a base language model can achieve comparable reasoning performance without extra training or datasets.
The primary technical hurdle identified is the need for an efficient sampler to effectively draw from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in RLHF: the reliance on static reward models which struggle with diverse and heterogeneous human preferences.
Existing multi-reward frameworks are limited because they cannot adapt to unseen preference domains without significant computational cost.
The proposed method introduces 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to improve the robustness of preference modeling in LLMs.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, which relies on externalizing intermediate thoughts (autoregressive generation).
The proposed method aims to mimic human cognition by using an internal 'working memory' mechanism for information manipulation.
This approach seeks to decouple internal computation from the external communication process to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: The research introduces 'COMPOSE' for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses a gap by simultaneously considering both the direction of prior work (grounding) and formal structural dependencies.
This represents an advancement in grounded future mathematical generation, moving beyond models that only consider one constraint.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method proposes achieving strong reasoning in language models by sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base model.
This approach potentially bypasses the need for expensive post-training techniques like Reinforcement Learning (RL) or curated datasets.
The practical implementation challenge lies in efficiently developing a sampler capable of converging to this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show unresolved comparisons on major leaderboards like Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and MMLU-Pro.
The paper proposes framing paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem to improve diagnostic capabilities.

stories.title: Associative learning turns DEET from aversive to appetitive in Aedes aegypti
stories.author: croes
bullet_points: Research explores using associative learning to change the perception of DEET.
The study focuses on modifying the response of Aedes aegypti mosquitoes.
The goal is to shift DEET from an aversive (repelling) stimulus to an appetitive (attractive) one.

stories.title: I Made My AI Models Argue, Then Let Hermes Be the Judge
stories.author: arqamwd
bullet_points: The article describes a $0 multi-model decision agent architecture.
It utilizes three Large Language Models (LLMs) in a debate format.
A specific LLM, 'Hermes,' is employed to act as the judge to determine trustworthiness.

stories.title: Claude vs Gemini Across 4 Security Domains: A Dead Heat — and the Hardening 63% of AI Code Skips
stories.author: ofri-peretz
bullet_points: A comparative security analysis pits Claude against Gemini across four specific security domains.
Both models showed parity in certain areas (JWT, MongoDB) but demonstrated specific strengths (Gemini in NestJS).
The core finding is that both frontier models exhibit a shared vulnerability related to the negative space static analysis they are designed to catch.

stories.title: Your AI writes PR descriptions from your commit messages. That's the bug.
stories.author: peterverse180
bullet_points: The article discusses the discrepancy between developer intent (commit messages) and actual code changes (diffs).
It highlights a potential pitfall or 'bug' in using AI tools to generate PR descriptions directly from commit messages.
The core theme is the drift between descriptive documentation and implemented reality in software development.

stories.title: I Built a Free AI Business Manager for Street Vendors in Hindi & English
stories.author: abhi3kg
bullet_points: Development of a free, AI-powered business manager specifically tailored for street vendors.
The tool features bilingual support in Hindi and English, addressing a specific regional need.
The project demonstrates practical, localized application of AI/ML in underserved markets.
